content.author: Sebastian Dawber
content.project_overview: FoundMe is a cloud-based platform enabling real-time goods tracking integrated with customs systems across the UK, Switzerland, and South Africa. The service provides import/export operations with complete visibility and automated compliance documentation.
content.problem_statement: Current import/export operations lack unified visibility across supply chains. Manual customs documentation processes create delays (average 5-7 days per shipment), compliance verification is fragmented, and audit trails are incomplete. SMEs cannot afford dedicated customs brokers.
content.primary_objective: Enable real-time GPS and documentary tracking for cross-border goods shipments.
content.secondary_objective: Reduce customs clearance time from 5-7 days to 24-48 hours through automated compliance validation.
content.additional_objective: Provide verifiable audit trails and regulatory compliance documentation.
content.solution_summary: FoundMe integrates GPS tracking, customs API connections, and AI-powered compliance engine. Shippers upload documentation; platform validates against regulatory requirements; customs authorities receive pre-cleared documentation; goods tracked in real-time.
content.market_1: United Kingdom - 2,500+ SME importers in food, hides, cannabis, and materials
content.market_2: Switzerland - 1,800+ SME importers with EU trade requirements
content.market_3: South Africa - 1,200+ SME exporters supplying UK and EU markets
content.category_1: Food and Caviar - High-value perishables requiring rapid clearance
content.category_2: Animal Hides and Clothing - Subject to CITES regulations and material certification
content.category_3: CBD and Medical Cannabis - Heavily regulated, requires compliance documentation
content.category_4: Building Materials - Subject to origin certification and environmental compliance
content.feature_1: Real-time GPS tracking with geofencing alerts for high-value shipments
content.feature_2: Automated customs documentation validation against HMRC, Swiss Customs, and SARS requirements
content.feature_3: Regulatory compliance engine checking GDPR, CITES, and phytosanitary requirements automatically
content.regulatory_considerations: GDPR compliance for data storage and transmission. CITES regulations for protected species. Phytosanitary certificates for agricultural goods. Customs regulations in UK, Switzerland, and South Africa.
content.constraints_assumptions: 12-month MVP delivery window. Estimated £800k Series A required. Assumes customs APIs remain stable. Requires partnerships with customs authorities in each market.
content.metric_1: 80% reduction in customs clearance time (from 5-7 days to 24-48 hours)
content.metric_2: Zero compliance violations across tracked shipments
content.action_1: Secure Series A funding (£800k) - Owner: CEO - Due: Q3 2026
content.action_2: Deploy MVP in UK market - Owner: Engineering - Due: Q4 2026
content.action_3: Expand to Switzerland and South Africa - Owner: Operations - Due: Q2 2027
